markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Bridge probationary claims and CRDT proposals into stable EventLedger-backed knowledge facts. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: ClaimPromotionBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ClaimPromotionBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 d81c79a6; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: AUTHORITY HOLE (high): promote_graph_proposal (claim_promotion.rs:194-210) copies proposal.source_span_refs verbatim into knowledge_crdt_promoted_facts (authority_class=authority) with NO span-existence re-validation. The migration 0152 header and 0153 header BOTH claim 'the promotion gate (MT-069) re-validates' span existence, but the implementer's own honest-risk note ('promotion does not re-validate span existence (soft refs)') is the TRUTH: there is no lookup against knowledge_spans anywhere in claim_promotion.rs or storage/knowledge_crdt.rs::insert_promoted_fact_idempotent (knowledge_crdt.rs:821-852). source_span_refs is JSONB with only a non-empty-array CHECK (0153 line 33-36) and NO FK to knowledge_spans. Therefore a proposal citing a non-existent ('pending:<src>:<range>'), retired, or foreign-workspace span id becomes durable authority with a dangling evidence ref. This violates spec 2.3.13.11 'KnowledgeClaim MUST carry ... evidence spans' for the record family the registry labels KnowledgeClaim (0153 line 58). The migration comment is a FALSE claim about the code.

CRASH WINDOW (high): the EventLedger PROMOTION_REQUESTED/ACCEPTED pair commits in its own transaction (postgres.rs:7414-7419 tx.commit) at claim_promotion.rs:181-184, and the authority fact insert is a SEPARATE statement at claim_promotion.rs:194-210. A crash in that window leaves the ledger saying 'promoted' with no fact row. Pure passive ledger replay does NOT reconstruct the fact (promoted_facts is independent authority state, not a replay projection materialized from PROMOTION_ACCEPTED). Convergence only on OPERATION RETRY: the idempotent guard at claim_promotion.rs:91 keys on the FACT row (get_promoted_fact_by_proposal), not the ledger; on retry it re-appends the pair (idempotency keys dedup to same event ids) then inserts the fact. So 'crashed/retried promotion converges' (header line 16-18) is true ONLY for active retry, FALSE for replay reconstruction. MT-077's 'idempotent replays converge on the same fact id' claim should be scoped to retry, not passive replay.

The fact_id is generated fresh (new_ulid, line 197) on every attempt but deduped by ON CONFLICT(proposal_id) DO NOTHING then re-read (knowledge_crdt.rs:849); the orphaned ULID is harmless. Not a defect, but note re-promotion after a crash burns a ULID.
evasion_probes: Searched claim_promotion.rs and storage/knowledge_crdt.rs for any knowledge_spans / KnowledgeSpan / span existence query feeding the promotion path: NONE found. The only span validation is the non-empty-string check at insert_graph_proposal (knowledge_crdt.rs:637-646), which runs at PROPOSAL time and accepts arbitrary strings.

Checked whether the FK on promotion_requested_event_id/promotion_accepted_event_id to kernel_event_ledger (0153 line 43-46) closes the window: it does NOT — it only guarantees the events exist when the fact is inserted, not that the fact is inserted when the events exist.

Checked gate-actor enforcement: promoted_by CHECK regex '^(operator|validator|system):...' (0153 line 41) + runtime kind gate (claim_promotion.rs:96-99) is sound; models cannot self-promote. This part holds.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs:7409-7420 (atomic event pair is its own tx; fact insert is not in it)
spec 02-system-architecture.md 2.3.13.11 line 2704 (Claims MUST carry evidence spans) and 2715 (promotion path)

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (33 CRDT tests pass under runtime-full,test-utils).

evidence: Hardened d6adf2dd: HIGH soft-ref hole CLOSED: promote_graph_proposal validates every KSP- span exists/same-workspace/not-retired (migration 0190 BEFORE INSERT trigger backstop); pending/missing/foreign/retired DENIED with durable receipt; promotion made atomic (promote_graph_fact_atomic single tx) + replay-converges. Negative tests must_refuse_promotion_citing_nonexistent_span + promotion_is_atomic_and_converges_after_crash_window PASS.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
